evidence.physics_rationale: Rear pushrod move changes static rake support and rear platform stability.
Rear wheel support balances traction retention against low-speed rotation support.
Front camber is tied to contact patch shape, carcass temperature, and understeer support.
Rear camber balances traction stability against tyre support and heat distribution.
Brake bias is adjusted from braking lock evidence and braking-platform stability.
TC slip target tracks the measured rear power-slip envelope.
Brake bias target is passed through from hardware/session context because the solver only solves static brake bias.
Brake migration is carried forward as hardware context; no migration model is currently solved by the pipeline.
Front master cylinder sizing is treated as pass-through hardware context rather than a solved setup output.
Rear master cylinder sizing is treated as pass-through hardware context rather than a solved setup output.
Pad compound is reported honestly as pass-through brake hardware context, not as a solved telemetry output.

constraints.name: Roll spring rate in range (190 N/mm)
RARB sensitivity within useful range
constraints.note: <0.005 = insensitive (wrong bar size), >0.05 = too sensitive (step down)

car_specific_notes: BMW M4 GT3 EVO: Front ARB 'D3-D3', Rear ARB 'D1-D1' (blade dimension collapsed — size label is the live tuning unit).
Live RARB walk: slow corners -> 'D1-D1' (softer for rotation), fast corners -> 'D2-D2' (stiffer for front bite via LLTD shift).
Cold tyre out-lap: softer rear ARB size to prevent snap oversteer.
NOTE: GT3 ARB stiffness tables are PENDING_IBT — sensitivity readings will be 0 until per-size stiffness is calibrated.

constraints.name: Front dynamic camber at peak g
Rear dynamic camber at peak g
Rear toe non-destabilizing
constraints.note: Rear toe-out is destabilizing — keep at or above 0mm

constraints.name: Front conditioning to op temp
constraints.note: Vision tread: >20 laps indicates tyres may not reach temp in a stint

notes: Representative cornering: 4.90g (p95=2.01g, peak=6.14g, kerb_weight=0.7). Roll: 3.0° at representative, 3.8° at peak.
Tyre temperature spread diagnosis: inner hotter = correct camber. If outer runs hotter -> reduce negative camber by 0.2-0.3 deg.
BMW M4 GT3 EVO: target tyre temp 85-105°C (Michelin GTP compound). Conditioning typically 10-15 laps from cold start.
For sprint qualifying: add 0.2-0.3° more negative camber + 0.2mm extra toe-out to accelerate thermal buildup.

supporting.brake_bias_reasoning: Calibrated base: 52.0% | Fuel correction: -0.02% at 80L | Result: 52.0%; +0.7% from trail brake depth p95=0.74; ⚠ High brake deceleration asymmetry (17.5 m/s²) detected — check front caliper/pad condition before adjusting brake bias; -0.2% for front brake wheel decel asymmetry p95=17.5 m/s^2; MC: F/R = 17.8/17.8 mm (ratio 1.00); Pad: Low friction — low brake temps, softer compound may improve initial bite
supporting.brake_hardware_status: Static brake bias is solved from telemetry; brake target, migration, master cylinders, and pad compound are preserved as legal seeded context.
supporting.diff_reasoning: Base: 100.0 Nm (car default 100 Nm; lat transfer=25038N at peak lat_g=6.14g); anchored to driver-loaded preload=100 Nm; clutch plates=8 | Coast 50 deg (light trail braker, less entry lock = rotation); Drive 70 deg (from throttle R^2=0.56) | Lock: coast=82.1% drive=47.1% (preload 20.4% + plates 61.6%)
supporting.tc_reasoning: Baseline: gain=4, slip=3; -1 gain for consistent driver; anchored to driver-loaded gain=4
supporting.pressure_reasoning: Track temp 32°C → cold adj -0.5 kPa; Front hot 177 kPa → cold 152 kPa; Rear hot 177 kPa → cold 152 kPa

warnings: Pushrod geometry not calibrated from garage data. Front/rear pushrod-to-RH sensitivity uses default estimates. Collect 3+ garage screenshots at different pushrod settings to calibrate.
